meta.title: Numerical Reasoning Test
meta.description: A skills-based test that evaluates your ability to handle data, graphs, percentages, ratios, and word problems across 10 areas of numerical reasoning. Each question has one correct answer.

levels.label: Excellent
levels.desc: You handle numerical data, percentages, and word problems with real confidence and accuracy. This is a strong, reliable skill for you.

levels.label: Strong
levels.desc: You solve most numerical reasoning problems correctly and efficiently. A little extra practice on trickier multi-step questions will sharpen this further.

levels.label: Good
levels.desc: You have a solid grasp of the fundamentals, but certain question types — like data interpretation or multi-step word problems — could use more practice.

levels.label: Developing
levels.desc: Your results are mixed — strong in some areas, shaky in others. Focused practice on your weaker subtopics will help a lot.

levels.label: Needs Practice
levels.desc: Numerical reasoning tasks are currently challenging for you. Regular practice with percentages, ratios, and basic word problems will build confidence.

levels.label: Needs Foundational Review
levels.desc: This is a good starting point — going back over basic arithmetic, percentages, and fractions will build the foundation for stronger numerical reasoning.

questions.text: What is 55% of 540?
120 is what percent of 200?
The price of a jacket is Rs. 500. It is increased by 10%. What is the new price?
questions.explanation: 10% of 500 = 50, so the new price is 500 + 50 = 550.

questions.text: A backpack priced at Rs. 460 is on sale with a 30% discount. What is the sale price?
questions.explanation: 30% of 460 = 138, so the sale price is 460 − 138 = 322.

questions.text: A class has 100 students in total. 65% of the students are girls. How many boys are in the class?

questions.explanation: 45% of 100 = 45, so the new price is 100 + 45 = 145.

questions.explanation: 30% of 260 = 78, so the sale price is 260 − 78 = 182.

Rs. 50 is divided between two friends in the ratio 1:4. What is the smaller share?
questions.explanation: Total parts = 1 + 4 = 5; each part = 50 ÷ 5 = 10; smaller share = 1 × 10 = 10.

questions.text: 3 workers can finish building a wall in 20 days. Working at the same rate, how many days will 5 workers take to finish the same wall?
questions.explanation: Total work = 3 × 20 = 60 worker-days; days for 5 workers = 60 ÷ 5 = 12.

questions.text: A recipe needs 4 cups of flour to make 5 servings. How many cups of flour are needed to make 15 servings?
Simplify the ratio 12:15 to its lowest terms.
questions.explanation: The greatest common factor of 12 and 15 is 1; dividing both terms by 3 gives 4:5.
